executive_summary.headline: OpenAI launches a $4B deployment company and Daybreak cybersecurity platform on the same day
executive_summary.body: On May 12, OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company — backed by $4 billion from 19 investment firms including Goldman Sachs, Bain, Capgemini, McKinsey, and TPG — to embed engineers directly inside enterprise clients, acquire operational workflows, and ship production deployments. The launch included the acquisition of Tomoro, an AI consulting firm that brings roughly 150 forward-deployed engineers under OpenAI. The same day, OpenAI unveiled Daybreak, a cybersecurity platform combining GPT-5.5-Cyber and Codex Security to build editable threat models from enterprise repositories, isolate and test vulnerabilities, and propose patches. Daybreak launched with eight major security partners: Akamai, Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, Fortinet, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler.

executive_summary.headline: SAP Sapphire 2026 delivers the Autonomous Enterprise: 50+ Joule Assistants, 200+ agents, €100M partner fund
executive_summary.body: At SAP Sapphire in Orlando (May 13–14), SAP CEO Christian Klein launched the Autonomous Enterprise — a unified SAP Business AI Platform combining Business Technology Platform, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation into a single governed environment. The platform includes Joule Studio (no-code and pro-code agent builder), more than 50 domain-specific Joule Assistants spanning Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, HCM, and CX, and an orchestrating layer of over 200 specialized agents. SAP launched a €100 million partner fund to accelerate deployment and new agent-led ERP migration tooling it claims can reduce transformation time by over 35%. Strategic AI partners include Anthropic (Claude in Joule agents for HR, procurement, and supply chain), NVIDIA (OpenShell runtime), Mistral AI and Cohere (sovereign model options), and n8n (workflow orchestration inside Joule Studio).

executive_summary.headline: Anthropic launches Claude for Small Business and brings Claude Platform to AWS as a GA service
executive_summary.body: Anthropic launched two distinct offerings during the week. Claude for Small Business (announced May 11, on tour from May 14) is a package of 15 ready-to-run agentic workflows built into Claude Cowork, connected to QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365 — with no additional fees beyond existing Claude and partner tool subscriptions. Claude Platform on AWS reached general availability on May 11 as the first cloud-provider-native deployment of Anthropic's full platform, giving AWS customers access to Managed Agents, Codex execution, MCP connectors, Skills, Files API, and web search through their existing IAM credentials and AWS billing — without a separate Anthropic account.

executive_summary.headline: Google previews Gemini Intelligence for Android at the Android Show: I/O Edition; Googlebook laptop and Aluminium OS debut
executive_summary.body: On May 12 at the Android Show: I/O Edition, Google unveiled Gemini Intelligence — proactive AI features built into Android that automate multi-step tasks across apps, including converting a grocery list photo into an active delivery cart, booking appointments in Chrome, filling out forms using personal context, and generating custom Android widgets from natural language. Google also debuted the Googlebook, a new laptop class running Aluminium OS (formerly discussed as a ChromeOS/Android merger) featuring Magic Pointer, an AI-enhanced cursor built with DeepMind for contextual on-screen suggestions. Rollout starts summer 2026 on Pixel and Samsung Galaxy devices, expanding to watches, cars, glasses, and laptops.

executive_summary.headline: Recursive Superintelligence emerges from stealth with $650M at $4.65B valuation; Anthropic in talks to raise $30B at ~$900B
executive_summary.body: Two funding signals bookended the week. Recursive Superintelligence, a four-month-old London startup founded by former Meta AI, Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Salesforce AI leaders (including Richard Socher and Yuandong Tian), emerged from stealth on May 13 with $650 million led by GV, Greycroft, Nvidia, and AMD — valued at $4.65 billion with fewer than 30 employees and no released product. Separately, Bloomberg reported on May 12–13 that Anthropic is in early talks to raise at least $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion, which would make it the world's most valuable private AI company. No term sheet has been signed; the round could close by end of May.

top_picks.title: OpenAI Deployment Company (DeployCo)
top_picks.subtitle: $4B backed by Goldman, McKinsey, Bain, Capgemini, TPG — 150 forward-deployed engineers via Tomoro acquisition
top_picks.summary: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company on May 12 — a standalone consulting and services unit backed by $4 billion from 19 investment firms, consultancies, and system integrators led by TPG and co-led by Advent, Bain Capital, and Brookfield. Founding partners include Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, Capgemini, Bain, SoftBank, Warburg Pincus, and WCAS. The unit acquired Tomoro, an AI consulting and engineering firm, giving it a base of roughly 150 forward-deployed engineers. DeployCo will embed directly inside client organizations to identify workflows, connect OpenAI models to internal systems, and ship production deployments — mirroring the model Anthropic launched with its Blackstone-backed services firm the week prior. Pre-money valuation of $10 billion; minimum investor return of 17.5%.
top_picks.competitive_context: DeployCo launches one week after Anthropic's Blackstone-backed enterprise services firm, creating a direct race between the two frontier labs for the embedded-engineer-at-enterprise model. The fact that McKinsey and Bain are co-investors in the firm competing against traditional consulting is a structural irony Axios explicitly flagged: these firms may be financing their own displacement. The PE co-lead structure (TPG, Advent, Brookfield) mirrors the Anthropic/Blackstone playbook, giving OpenAI access to a comparable captive customer pipeline through portfolio companies. Accenture, Deloitte, and IBM Global Services are the incumbent SIs being squeezed by both launches simultaneously.
top_picks.pricing: Enterprise service firm; project-based pricing not publicly disclosed. $4 billion in committed capital funds the embedded engineering model. Investors receive a minimum 17.5% return with profit limits.
key_takeaways: Both OpenAI and Anthropic now operate PE-backed embedded engineering firms launched within 8 days of each other — the market for enterprise AI implementation is being institutionalized at scale.
The Tomoro acquisition gives DeployCo 150 operational engineers immediately, bypassing the 12–18 month ramp that new consulting practices normally require.

McKinsey and Bain as co-investors means they receive privileged access to OpenAI's product roadmap while building their own practices on top — a conflict of interest that traditional clients should factor into procurement decisions.

The $10B pre-money valuation and 17.5% minimum return floor signal a structured private equity deal, not a venture round — investors are pricing stable enterprise services cash flows, not startup upside.
Enterprise teams without in-house AI engineering should expect direct outreach from DeployCo in the near term, particularly those whose investors are among the 19 backing partners.

top_picks.title: SAP Autonomous Enterprise — Joule Studio, 50+ Assistants, 200+ Agents
top_picks.subtitle: SAP Business AI Platform unifies BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation; €100M partner fund at Sapphire 2026
top_picks.summary: At SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando, SAP launched the Autonomous Enterprise: a unified SAP Business AI Platform (merging Business Technology Platform, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation) and an Autonomous Suite deploying more than 50 Joule Assistants and over 200 specialized agents across Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, HCM, and CX. Joule Studio is the no-code/pro-code agent builder on SAP-managed infrastructure. The Autonomous Close Assistant can compress financial close from weeks to days by automating journal entries, reconciliation, and error resolution. RISE with SAP customers get three assistants activated in year one; SAP GROW customers receive full portfolio access at onboarding. ERP migration tooling claims 35%+ reduction in transformation effort. SAP launched a €100 million partner fund and added strategic AI integrations with Anthropic, NVIDIA (OpenShell runtime), Mistral AI, Cohere, n8n, and Google Cloud.
top_picks.competitive_context: SAP's Autonomous Enterprise is the most consequential ERP vendor AI launch since Salesforce Agentforce. The platform directly challenges Microsoft's Dynamics 365 Copilot and Oracle Fusion AI on the enterprise ERP layer. The Anthropic Claude integration for Joule agents (HR, procurement, supply chain) means SAP customers running RISE with SAP gain Claude access without a separate Anthropic procurement relationship. The KPMG deployment of 20 agents targeting $120 million in contract leakage reduction is a customer-reported production figure, not a pilot — placing SAP among a small group of vendors with measurable enterprise AI ROI at scale. The Mistral AI and Cohere sovereign model options are a specific response to European data residency requirements in regulated sectors.
top_picks.pricing: SAP Business AI Platform is included in RISE with SAP and SAP GROW (three assistants activated in year one for RISE; full portfolio access for GROW). On-premises S/4HANA and SAP ECC customers must commit to cloud migration to access AI scenarios. Joule Studio agent-building pricing not separately disclosed. €100 million partner fund available to partners building or extending agents on Joule Studio.
key_takeaways: Any organization running SAP ERP in a regulated environment now has a direct path to 200+ pre-built agents — without building an AI platform from scratch.
Anthropic Claude in Joule Assistants means SAP's AI layer inherits Claude's safety features, which matters for regulated HR, procurement, and financial workflows.
The Autonomous Close Assistant (journal entries, reconciliation, error resolution) targets the highest-cost manual process in finance — an audit-ready production deployment, not a demo.
KPMG's $120M contract leakage target from 20 agents at a single client is the clearest ROI signal in the week's enterprise AI launches.
On-premises and ECC customers must commit to cloud migration to access AI scenarios — SAP is using the AI capability gap as a modernization forcing function.

top_picks.title: OpenAI Daybreak — Enterprise Cybersecurity Platform
top_picks.subtitle: GPT-5.5-Cyber, Codex Security, editable threat models, and eight major security partners at launch
top_picks.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak on May 12, a cybersecurity platform combining GPT-5.5 (standard), GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access for Cyber (verified defensive work), and GPT-5.5-Cyber (red teaming, penetration testing, controlled validation). Codex Security builds an editable threat model of a given repository, focusing analysis on realistic attack paths, then isolates and tests likely vulnerabilities before proposing fixes. The final stage delivers audit-ready evidence back into enterprise systems for tracking and remediation. Launch partners under the Trusted Access for Cyber initiative include Akamai, Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, Fortinet, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler. Unlike Anthropic's Claude Mythos, Daybreak is publicly available — enterprises can request a vulnerability scan directly via OpenAI's sales team.
top_picks.competitive_context: Daybreak is OpenAI's direct answer to Anthropic's Claude Mythos, which found thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities in a limited preview. Daybreak's key differentiator is public accessibility — Mythos remains in restricted preview, while Daybreak allows any organization to request a repository scan. The three-tier model structure (standard, Trusted Access, full Cyber) gives enterprise security teams a graduated on-ramp that matches their authorization level. Gartner analyst John Watts positions Daybreak as competing more directly with application security posture management (ASPM) tools than traditional SIEM/EDR products — a newer, less crowded market. The European Commission's parallel discussions with OpenAI about government access signal potential public-sector expansion.
top_picks.pricing: Access by request — enterprises must contact OpenAI sales or request a vulnerability scan. GPT-5.5-Cyber tier access remains tightly controlled at launch with unspecified restrictions for authorized environments.
key_takeaways: Unlike Anthropic's Mythos, Daybreak is publicly accessible via sales request — a security team can request a scan without waiting for a restricted preview.
The three-tier model structure means regulated enterprises can use standard GPT-5.5 for developer assistance and escalate to Trusted Access for Cyber for authorized defensive work.
Audit-ready evidence output in stage three is the governance feature that compliance teams need before approving AI-assisted vulnerability remediation in a regulated environment.
Eight launch partners (Cisco, CrowdStrike, Cloudflare, Palo Alto Networks, Zscaler, Fortinet, Oracle, Akamai) means Daybreak integrates into the security stack enterprises already use.
Codex Security's editable threat model approach means human security teams review and modify the AI's attack path analysis before any patch is generated — human-in-the-loop by design.

top_picks.title: Claude Platform on AWS — General Availability
top_picks.subtitle: Anthropic's full native platform through AWS IAM, billing, and CloudTrail — first cloud-provider deployment of its kind
top_picks.summary: On May 11, AWS announced the general availability of Claude Platform on AWS — a new service that gives customers direct access to Anthropic's complete native platform experience using existing AWS IAM credentials, AWS Marketplace consumption billing, and CloudTrail audit logging. No separate Anthropic account, credentials, or billing relationship is required. The service includes the full Claude API, Claude Managed Agents (beta), web search and fetch, MCP connector (beta), Agent Skills (beta), code execution, and Files API (beta). Available across 17 AWS commercial regions globally. This complements — and differs from — Claude on Amazon Bedrock: Claude Platform on AWS is operated by Anthropic and processes data outside the AWS security boundary, making it suited for teams without specific regional data residency requirements.
top_picks.competitive_context: AWS is the first cloud provider to offer native Claude Platform access — this removes the single most common enterprise friction point (managing a separate Anthropic vendor relationship, contract, and billing). Teams that already have AWS enterprise agreements now have a path to Claude Managed Agents, MCP connectors, and code execution without new procurement. The distinction from Amazon Bedrock is critical: Bedrock processes data within AWS's security boundary; Claude Platform on AWS does not. Enterprise security teams must evaluate data residency requirements before choosing between the two. Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Vertex AI already provide Claude model access — but neither yet offers the full native Anthropic platform experience including Managed Agents.
top_picks.pricing: Consumption billing through AWS Marketplace. Pricing tracks Anthropic's standard Claude API rates via AWS billing; specific per-token pricing inherits from the Anthropic pricing page. No separate Anthropic subscription or minimum commitment required.
key_takeaways: AWS IAM authentication means existing enterprise identity governance policies (MFA, role-based access, federated IdP) apply automatically to Claude Platform access.
CloudTrail audit logging provides the activity trace that regulated enterprises need before approving AI workloads — available from day one without additional configuration.
Managed Agents in beta means enterprise teams can begin testing agent orchestration workflows inside their existing AWS account before the feature reaches GA.
The 17-region availability list covers most enterprise AWS deployments globally, including EU Frankfurt, EU Paris, and APAC, though specific data residency SLAs differ from Bedrock.
Teams needing strict AWS data boundary guarantees should use Amazon Bedrock; Claude Platform on AWS is the better path for teams that prioritize access to Anthropic's latest native features.

top_picks.title: Claude for Small Business
top_picks.subtitle: 15 ready-to-run agentic workflows inside QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365
top_picks.summary: Anthropic launched Claude for Small Business on May 13 — a package of 15 ready-to-run agentic workflows built into Claude Cowork that connects Claude to seven platforms small businesses already use: Intuit QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365. Workflows span finance (payroll planning, month-end close, cash forecasting), operations (contract review, tax prep), sales (lead triage), marketing (campaign creation, content generation), HR, and customer service. Claude does not act autonomously: users initiate each workflow and approve before anything sends, posts, or pays. Existing account permissions hold — Claude cannot access anything the connected account cannot. No additional fees beyond existing Claude subscription and partner tool costs. Ten-city US training tour starts May 14 in Chicago; attendees receive one month of Claude Max.
top_picks.competitive_context: Claude for Small Business competes with Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace AI features for the sub-100-employee market. The key differentiator is cross-platform workflow orchestration: Claude can simultaneously pull cash position from QuickBooks, match against PayPal settlements, draft a P&L, and send the packet to an accountant — crossing app boundaries that single-vendor Copilot products cannot span. The no-additional-cost pricing (add-on to existing Claude subscription) undercuts both Microsoft and Google's per-user AI pricing. The human-approval-before-action design directly addresses the data security concern cited by half of small business survey respondents, making it relevant for compliance-aware teams.
top_picks.pricing: No additional fees for Claude for Small Business. Included with existing Claude Team or Enterprise subscription. Partner tool subscriptions (QuickBooks, HubSpot, etc.) are separate. Ten-city training tour is free for attendees, who receive one month of Claude Max.
key_takeaways: The human-in-the-loop approval design means Claude for Small Business does not introduce autonomous financial or operational actions without explicit user authorization — a direct response to the top cited concern from small business surveys.
Cross-app workflow orchestration (QuickBooks + PayPal + HubSpot simultaneously) is the capability that single-vendor Microsoft or Google AI add-ons cannot replicate without custom integration work.
The zero-additional-cost pricing removes the budget justification barrier that has slowed AI adoption at sub-100-employee organizations.
Anthropic's 'no training on customer data by default on Team and Enterprise plans' policy addresses the data concern without requiring enterprise contracts.
The ten-city tour signals a direct-to-SMB distribution strategy that competes with Microsoft's partner channel and Google Workspace resellers — a new go-to-market motion for a lab-origin company.

top_picks.title: Recursive Superintelligence — $650M Stealth Emergence
top_picks.subtitle: GV, Greycroft, Nvidia, AMD back self-improving AI startup at $4.65B; no product yet, 4 months old
top_picks.summary: Recursive Superintelligence, a four-month-old London-based AI startup, emerged from stealth on May 13 with $650 million at a $4.65 billion valuation. The round was led by GV (Google Ventures) and Greycroft, with Nvidia and AMD as strategic backers. Founded by Richard Socher (former chief scientist at Salesforce) and Yuandong Tian (former director at Meta AI FAIR), alongside veterans from Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Uber AI, the company is pursuing recursive self-improvement: AI systems that autonomously improve themselves in an accelerating loop. The first target is training a system with the equivalent capabilities of '50,000 doctors' to automate AI scientific research. A public launch is targeted for mid-2026. The company has fewer than 30 employees and has not shipped a product.
top_picks.competitive_context: GV and Nvidia co-investing signals strategic alignment with Google and Nvidia's infrastructure businesses — both would benefit from a recursive self-improvement system that could accelerate model development on their infrastructure. At $4.65B valuation with no product and fewer than 30 employees, the valuation-to-output ratio is extreme even by 2026 standards. Direct competitors pursuing self-improving AI include Anthropic (Constitutional AI + iterative training), DeepMind (AlphaFold-era recursive improvement), and Scale AI (data flywheel for continuous model improvement). The near-term practical risk for enterprises: if recursive self-improvement approaches work, model capability timelines compress further, requiring faster procurement and governance review cycles.
top_picks.pricing: No product; no pricing disclosed. Funded company with $650M to fund compute infrastructure for recursive training experiments.
key_takeaways: GV and Nvidia co-leading a $650M round into a four-month-old company with no product is a direct signal that both organizations believe recursive self-improvement is technically achievable in the near term.
The '$50,000 doctors equivalent' target for AI scientific research automation is the near-term product thesis — not a commercial enterprise offering but a research automation system.
Public launch targeted for mid-2026 means enterprise procurement teams have a roughly six-month window before the company releases something evaluable.

The founding team's pedigree (Meta AI FAIR director, Salesforce chief scientist, DeepMind / OpenAI veterans) has historically been sufficient to attract institutional capital at pre-product stage in this cycle.
This is a MONITOR — not a near-term enterprise decision — but the recursive self-improvement thesis has implications for model capability timelines that should inform multi-year AI strategy planning.

top_picks.title: Google Gemini Intelligence for Android — Proactive Agentic OS Layer
top_picks.subtitle: Multi-step task automation across apps, Googlebook laptop with Aluminium OS, Magic Pointer; rollout starts summer 2026
top_picks.summary: At the Android Show: I/O Edition on May 12, Google unveiled Gemini Intelligence — proactive AI features embedded in Android that automate multi-step tasks across apps, including converting a grocery list photo into an active delivery cart, booking appointments in Chrome, filling forms using personal context, and generating custom Android widgets from natural language descriptions. Google debuted the Googlebook laptop running Aluminium OS with Magic Pointer, an AI-enhanced cursor built alongside DeepMind that provides contextual suggestions dynamically on-screen. Rollout starts summer 2026 on Pixel and Samsung Galaxy devices, then expands to watches, cars, glasses, and laptops. A sneak peek at Android XR glasses was shown with a confirmed 2026 hardware launch.
top_picks.competitive_context: Gemini Intelligence positions Android as an agent-executing OS layer — the same strategic territory Microsoft occupies with Windows Copilot and Apple with Intelligence on iOS/macOS. The Aluminium OS Googlebook competes directly with Microsoft Surface Copilot+ PCs and Apple MacBook with Intelligence. Magic Pointer's contextual on-screen suggestions are the most visible direct competitor to Microsoft's Windows Copilot sidebar. The enterprise implication: as Gemini Intelligence expands to enterprise Chrome OS devices, Google Workspace customers will gain ambient AI automation across apps without deploying a separate AI product. Full enterprise rollout timeline for managed device fleets is not yet confirmed.
top_picks.pricing: Gemini Intelligence is included in Android without separate charge. Aluminium OS and Googlebook pricing not announced. Google AI Pro and AI Ultra subscription benefits extend to Googlebook.
key_takeaways: Multi-step task automation across apps — not just in-app AI — is the architectural signal: Android is becoming an agent execution environment, not just a hosting layer for AI features.

The Aluminium OS Googlebook is the first laptop-class product merging Android and Chrome OS under a single Gemini-powered interface — enterprise IT should monitor for MDM implications as the new OS reaches managed device programs.

Android XR glasses with a confirmed 2026 hardware launch signal an ambient AI device category arriving within the year — relevant for enterprise use cases involving field workers and hands-free guidance.
Summer 2026 rollout on Pixel and Samsung Galaxy means the initial wave hits personal devices before enterprise managed fleet deployment — procurement decisions will follow adoption patterns.
Google I/O (May 19–20) is expected to provide more model details, enterprise feature specifics, and deployment timelines — defer major Google AI strategy decisions until after that event.

sections.title: Product Hunt: Top AI Launches
sections.description: Top AI and developer tool launches from Product Hunt daily leaderboards and the weekly leaderboard for May 11–17, 2026. Maker websites used as primary_url per resolution rules; PH product pages in secondary_urls.

items.title: OpenHuman
items.subtitle: Local-first personal AI agent with 118+ integrations, open-source — #1 for the week
items.summary: OpenHuman is an open-source, local-first desktop AI agent built by tinyhumans.ai. Two-minute setup; no config files; a simple GUI intended for non-technical users. Connects Gmail, Slack, Telegram, Notion, and GitHub in one click, with 118+ integrations total. Includes full tool calling with chaining and retries, a built-in code sandbox, and a desktop mascot that joins Google Meets, takes notes, and follows up afterward. All data stays on the user's machine. Trended #3 on Product Hunt during launch and #1 on GitHub Trending worldwide simultaneously. Topped the weekly leaderboard for May 11–17.

items.title: Spellar 3.0
items.subtitle: AI meeting companion with cross-meeting memory — #2 for the week, #1 on May 14 with 517 upvotes
items.summary: Spellar 3.0 is an AI meeting companion that records calls without a bot joiner, builds cross-meeting memory (ask what a client said three calls ago and get an accurate answer), organizes context by client and project, and lets users choose their AI backend — OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, or Gemini. Version 3.0 adds cross-meeting memory as the core differentiator over standard meeting recorders. Supports 100+ languages. Launched May 14 at #1 on Product Hunt with 517 upvotes.

items.title: articuler.ai
items.subtitle: Intent-based professional matching across 980M profiles — #1 on May 11
items.summary: Articuler matches professionals by intent rather than keyword search, across 980 million public profiles. Users describe their goal (investor looking for a check, hire needed for a roadmap, partner for a new market), and Articuler finds the matching professional, decodes them from their public footprint, and drafts a first outreach note. Claims a 15% reply rate and 8x cold outreach effectiveness. Won #1 on Product Hunt May 11.

items.title: Warp Open-Source
items.subtitle: Agentic development environment built with the community — #7 on May 11
items.summary: Warp launched its agentic development environment as open source in late April and surfaced on the May 11 Product Hunt leaderboard at #7. The open-source release positions Warp as a community-built ADE (Agentic Development Environment) — Rust-based terminal with AI and team knowledge built in. The open-source release enables enterprise teams to audit the codebase, fork it, or contribute to agent capabilities without commercial lock-in.

items.title: Graphbit PRFlow
items.subtitle: AI code reviewer with cross-file dependency tracing — #3 on May 11
items.summary: PRFlow indexes a codebase, traces cross-file dependencies, and produces a structured security-oriented code review within one to three minutes, automatically on every GitHub pull request. It posts structured review comments and allows developers to ask follow-up questions in the PR thread. Uses Graphbit Coins per-PR pricing: approximately 1,500 coins per review; Core batch at $25 for 1,000 coins. Launched #3 on Product Hunt May 11.

items.title: ClawSecure
items.subtitle: AI antivirus for AI agents — pre-install scanning, runtime monitoring, OWASP ASI coverage — #5 on May 11
items.summary: ClawSecure is a security scanner for AI agent packages — specifically targeting the AI Claw ecosystem. It provides pre-install scanning, real-time runtime monitoring via its Watchtower feature, an AI CISO Security Agent, and a sub-200ms Verification API. Claims full 10/10 OWASP ASI coverage. The company's audit of 2,890+ AI agent skills found 41% have security vulnerabilities; 1 in 5 send data to external servers. Free, no sign-up required. Second ClawSecure Product Hunt launch; the company previously hit #2 Product of the Day. Launched at #5 on May 11.

items.title: Wispr Flow
items.subtitle: AI dictation that writes in your style across every app — #1 on May 12 and May 13
items.summary: Wispr Flow is a Mac dictation app that transcribes natural speech, matches the user's writing style, and enters text into any application. Features include blazing-fast dictation, tone-matching, auto-edits when users change their mind mid-sentence, Command Mode for operating on selected text, native integrations with key productivity apps, whispering mode, and private-by-design local recording. Won #1 Product Hunt Launch of the Day on both May 12 and May 13.

items.title: Latitude for Claude Code
items.subtitle: Token-level session tracing for Claude Code — see every prompt, tool call, and cost per turn — #13 for the week
items.summary: Latitude for Claude Code adds full session tracing to Claude Code — capturing the complete system prompt, all tool calls, subagent delegations, and token consumption per turn. Setup is one command (npx -y @latitude-data/claude-code-telemetry install). Works in terminal, Claude Desktop, and IDEs. Traces are stored in the user's own Latitude account; nothing is sent externally. Weekly reports provide ongoing token usage analytics. Free to use. Specifically addresses the problem of teams hitting Claude Code rate limits without visibility into what consumed their quota.

sections.title: Frontier Models
sections.description: New model releases and model-tier updates from major labs during the week of May 11–17, 2026.

items.title: xAI Grok Build — First AI Coding Agent from xAI
items.subtitle: Planning mode, CLI, plugin compatibility — beta for SuperGrok Heavy subscribers at $300/month
items.summary: xAI launched Grok Build in beta on May 15 — its first AI coding agent, described as 'a robust coding assistant and CLI designed for professional software development and intricate coding tasks.' Features include a planning mode where users review, modify, and approve a project plan before implementation; compatibility with existing plugins and workflows. Access is currently limited to SuperGrok Heavy plan subscribers at $300/month. xAI acknowledged it has fallen behind Anthropic Claude Code and OpenAI Codex in this category; Grok Build is the direct response. Limited details provided at launch.

items.title: Google Gemini Intelligence — Android OS AI Layer
items.subtitle: Proactive multi-step task automation across apps; Aluminium OS debut with Magic Pointer
items.summary: At the Android Show: I/O Edition on May 12, Google unveiled Gemini Intelligence for Android — proactive agentic AI features that automate multi-step tasks across apps including converting a grocery list photo to a delivery cart, booking appointments in Chrome, and generating widgets from natural language. The Googlebook laptop with Aluminium OS and Magic Pointer (AI-enhanced cursor, built with DeepMind) debuted alongside. See Top Picks for full analysis.

items.title: Anthropic — In Talks to Raise $30B at ~$900B Valuation
items.subtitle: Bloomberg: round could close end of May; no term sheet signed as of May 17
items.summary: Bloomberg reported on May 12–13 that Anthropic is in early talks to raise at least $30 billion in fresh financing at a valuation exceeding $900 billion — which would make it the world's most valuable private AI company, surpassing OpenAI's $852B pre-money valuation from its January 2026 raise. The round could close by end of May. No term sheet has been signed. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow noted investors approached Anthropic after weeks of the company declining overtures; the $30–35 billion figure reflects what Anthropic now sees as strategically sensible given its $30B+ ARR run rate.

sections.title: YC and Startups
sections.description: Notable early-stage and YC-backed AI launches during the week of May 11–17, 2026.

items.title: Recursive Superintelligence — $650M at $4.65B Valuation
items.subtitle: GV, Greycroft, Nvidia, AMD back self-improving AI research startup; 4 months old, no product
items.summary: Recursive Superintelligence emerged from stealth with $650 million at a $4.65 billion valuation — four months after founding, with fewer than 30 employees and no released product. Founded by Richard Socher and Yuandong Tian with veterans from Meta AI FAIR, Google DeepMind, OpenAI, Salesforce AI, and Uber AI. The thesis is recursive self-improvement: AI systems that autonomously improve themselves. First target: training a system with '50,000 doctors equivalent' capability to automate AI scientific research, with a public launch targeted mid-2026. See Top Picks for full analysis.

items.title: Isomorphic Labs — $2.1B Series B
items.subtitle: Google DeepMind spinout closes AI drug discovery round; Thrive Capital leads, Alphabet participates
items.summary: Isomorphic Labs, the AI drug discovery company founded by Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, closed a $2.1 billion Series B led by Thrive Capital with participation from Alphabet, GV, MGX, Temasek, and the UK Sovereign AI Fund. The round ranks as the largest venture funding in biopharma since at least 2022. Isomorphic will use the capital to expand its drug pipeline toward clinical testing, building on Nobel Prize-winning AlphaFold technology. The company has existing partnerships with Novartis, Eli Lilly, and Johnson & Johnson.

items.title: OpenHuman — Open-Source Local-First AI Agent Desktop
items.subtitle: #1 Product Hunt week of May 11; #1 GitHub Trending worldwide simultaneously
items.summary: OpenHuman is an open-source desktop AI agent from tinyhumans.ai — local-first, no cloud dependency, no vendor lock-in. Two-minute setup via a GUI accessible to non-technical users. 118+ integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, GitHub, Stripe, Calendar, Drive, Linear) via one-click OAuth with auto-refresh every 20 minutes. Built-in code sandbox; a desktop mascot that joins Google Meets, takes notes, and follows up. Trended #1 on GitHub Trending worldwide and #1 Product Hunt week of May 11–17.

sections.title: Enterprise AI
sections.description: Enterprise platform and cloud AI announcements during the week of May 11–17, 2026.

items.title: OpenAI Deployment Company — Enterprise Embedding Model
items.subtitle: $4B, 19 investors, Tomoro acquisition, 150 forward-deployed engineers
items.summary: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company on May 12 — a $4 billion consulting and services unit backed by 19 firms including Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, Bain, Capgemini, SoftBank, and TPG. The acquisition of Tomoro provides 150 forward-deployed engineers from day one. See Top Picks for full analysis.

items.title: SAP Autonomous Enterprise — Joule Assistants GA at Sapphire 2026
items.subtitle: 50+ assistants, 200+ agents, €100M partner fund, Anthropic and NVIDIA integrations
items.summary: SAP introduced the Autonomous Enterprise at Sapphire 2026 — a unified AI platform with 50+ domain-specific Joule Assistants and 200+ specialized agents across Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, HCM, and CX. Agent-led ERP migration tooling claims 35%+ reduction in transformation effort. See Top Picks for full analysis.

items.title: Claude Platform on AWS — GA
items.subtitle: Full Anthropic native platform through AWS IAM, CloudTrail, and billing — first cloud-provider GA
items.summary: AWS reached general availability on May 11 for Claude Platform on AWS — the first cloud-provider native deployment of Anthropic's full platform, including Managed Agents, MCP connectors, code execution, and Files API, through AWS IAM and CloudTrail. See Top Picks for full analysis.

items.title: OpenAI Daybreak — Enterprise Vulnerability Detection Platform
items.subtitle: GPT-5.5-Cyber plus Codex Security; eight security partners at launch; available by request
items.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak on May 12 — a cybersecurity platform combining GPT-5.5-Cyber and Codex Security to build editable repository threat models, isolate and test vulnerabilities in sandboxed environments, and deliver audit-ready evidence for remediation. Available by direct request. Launch partners: Cisco, CrowdStrike, Cloudflare, Palo Alto Networks, Zscaler, Fortinet, Akamai, Oracle. See Top Picks for full analysis.

sections.title: Funding
sections.description: Notable AI funding rounds announced or confirmed during the week of May 11–17, 2026. All figures cross-checked against primary sources.

items.title: OpenAI Deployment Company — $4B (TPG, Advent, Bain Capital, Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, SoftBank + 13 others)
items.subtitle: $10B pre-money valuation; 19 investor firms; Tomoro acquisition included
items.summary: OpenAI's Deployment Company raised $4 billion from 19 investment firms led by TPG and co-led by Advent, Bain Capital, and Brookfield. $10B pre-money valuation. See Top Picks and Enterprise AI sections for full context.

items.title: Recursive Superintelligence — $650M (GV, Greycroft, Nvidia, AMD)
items.subtitle: $4.65B valuation; 4 months old; self-improving AI thesis
items.summary: Recursive Superintelligence raised $650M at $4.65B, backed by GV, Greycroft, Nvidia, and AMD. See Top Picks and YC & Startups sections for analysis.

items.title: Isomorphic Labs — $2.1B Series B (Thrive Capital, Alphabet, GV, MGX, Temasek, UK Sovereign AI Fund)
items.subtitle: Google DeepMind spinout; AI drug discovery; largest biopharma VC round since 2022
items.summary: Isomorphic Labs closed $2.1B Series B led by Thrive Capital with Alphabet, GV, MGX, Temasek, and UK Sovereign AI Fund. The largest single biopharma venture round since 2022. See YC & Startups section for details.

items.title: Anthropic — In Talks: $30B Round at ~$900B Valuation (Bloomberg report)
items.subtitle: No term sheet signed; could close end of May; would surpass OpenAI as world's most valuable private AI company
items.summary: Bloomberg reported Anthropic is in early talks to raise at least $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion — deal not final, no term sheet signed. Bloomberg URL returned 403 (paywall), but The Next Web and Yahoo Finance corroborate the Bloomberg report details. Marked unverified because the primary source is paywalled; the information is sourced from Bloomberg's anonymized reporting, not a company announcement.

sections.title: Open Source
sections.description: Notable open-source and developer tool releases during the week of May 11–17, 2026.

items.title: OpenHuman — Open-Source Desktop AI Agent
items.subtitle: #1 GitHub Trending worldwide and #1 Product Hunt week of May 11
items.summary: OpenHuman is a local-first open-source desktop AI agent — all data on the user's machine, no cloud dependency, no vendor lock-in. 118+ third-party integrations via OAuth with auto-refresh. Desktop mascot that joins Google Meets and follows up after the call. Trended simultaneously at #1 GitHub Trending worldwide and #1 Product Hunt week of May 11–17. Repository available at github.com/tinyhumans-ai/openhuman.

items.title: Kimi WebBridge
items.subtitle: Agent-agnostic browser operator: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Hermes, Kimi Code CLI; local data processing
items.summary: Moonshot AI launched Kimi WebBridge, a browser extension that gives AI agents eyes and hands inside Chrome or Edge. Agents can click, scroll, type, fill forms, and complete end-to-end tasks using live web pages. Processes all data locally — no cloud dependency for the browser operation layer. Supports multiple AI ecosystems: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Hermes, and Kimi Code CLI. Kimi K2.6 (multimodal) allows the agent to see pages visually, not just parse HTML. Setup via the Kimi desktop app plus a Chrome store extension.

sections.title: To Watch
sections.description: Early-stage signals with high potential, or confirmed events expected to produce launches next week.

items.title: Google I/O 2026 — May 19–20
items.subtitle: Expected: Gemini 4.0 (or major 3.x upgrade), Android XR glasses launch timeline, Aluminium OS hardware details
items.summary: Google I/O runs May 19–20 in Mountain View. The Android Show pre-event (May 12) delivered Gemini Intelligence and the Googlebook. I/O is expected to deliver the next Gemini model version (likely Gemini 4.0 or a major 3.x update), definitive Android XR glasses hardware details and timeline, Aluminium OS final specs, and deeper enterprise AI capability disclosures. Google Health and Fitbit Air also launch May 19. The week of May 18 will produce multiple pre-I/O announcement leaks — monitor io.google and blog.google for session schedule.
items.recurrence_note: Google I/O first flagged in the May 4 launches tab as a confirmed upcoming event. This week's Android Show pre-event delivered Gemini Intelligence; I/O (May 19–20) is the main event.

items.title: Anthropic $30B+ Round — Potential Close by End of May
items.subtitle: Bloomberg: round could close before June; no term sheet signed as of May 17
items.summary: Anthropic is in early talks to raise at least $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion. The round could close by end of May. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow noted investors were actively pushing Anthropic to accept capital for weeks before the company engaged. If closed, the $30 billion round at $900B+ would make Anthropic the world's most valuable private AI company. Track anthropic.com/news for announcement.

flagged_unverified.reason: Bloomberg primary source returns 403 (paywall). The information is reported by Bloomberg anonymized sourcing, corroborated by Yahoo Finance and The Next Web summarizing Bloomberg's reporting. No Anthropic press release or term sheet. Listed in funding section with verified: false and a note explaining the paywalled source.

sources_to_add: SAP News Center (news.sap.com) — SAP Sapphire 2026 delivered the week's most enterprise-relevant platform launch. Add news.sap.com to the enterprise AI source rotation alongside AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud blogs.
Tech.eu — Recursive Superintelligence's stealth emergence was broken by Tech.eu, not TechCrunch or VentureBeat. Add tech.eu to the European AI funding source list.

The SysAdmin Desk (thesysadmindesk.com/ainews) — Enterprise AI Weekly for May 12–18 was a useful weekly synthesis with verified detail on OpenAI DeployCo, SAP, and Anthropic. Add as a secondary synthesis source to cross-check against primary announcements.
topics_to_add: AI services firm models — OpenAI and Anthropic both launched PE-backed embedded-engineer services firms within 8 days of each other. Track for Google, Microsoft, and Meta equivalents. The race to own enterprise implementation is now a distinct product category.

Recursive self-improvement / self-improving AI — Recursive Superintelligence's emergence with $650M is the first institutional-scale bet on recursive self-improvement as a near-term commercial thesis. Track for product releases in the mid-2026 window.

AI-native ERP and enterprise platform agents — SAP's Autonomous Enterprise with 200+ specialized agents embedded in production ERP workflows is a category milestone. Track Oracle Fusion AI, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Copilot, and Salesforce Agentforce for comparable launches in the coming weeks.

Browser automation agents as AI infrastructure — Kimi WebBridge and similar browser-control extensions are becoming a standard layer for AI agent access to legacy web applications. Track for enterprise-grade equivalents with audit trails and access controls.
suggested_refinements.notes: The week of May 11–17 was defined by the institutionalization of enterprise AI deployment. Two of the three largest launches were services businesses, not products: OpenAI DeployCo ($4B, 150 engineers, 19 PE/consulting investors) and — trailing from the prior week — Anthropic's Blackstone-backed services firm. Both take the same approach: embed engineers from the model lab directly inside mid-to-large enterprises to identify workflows and ship production deployments. The strategic signal is that model capability is no longer the bottleneck; the bottleneck is implementation bandwidth inside regulated enterprises with complex legacy systems. SAP's Autonomous Enterprise launch is the counterpoint: the incumbent ERP vendor is packaging the same answer as a platform product, so enterprises that are already on SAP's cloud stack may not need a separate services firm. OpenAI Daybreak and Claude Platform on AWS represent the infrastructure layer maturing: Daybreak brings AI into the security operations workflow with audit-ready evidence; Claude Platform on AWS eliminates the separate vendor relationship barrier for AWS-first enterprises. The Recursive Superintelligence raise ($650M for a 4-month-old company with no product) is a speculative outlier — but the GV and Nvidia co-investment is a directional signal worth tracking. The week's Product Hunt leaderboard was relatively early-stage, with OpenHuman's local-first architecture the clearest builder-adjacent signal: enterprises interested in auditable, non-cloud-dependent agent deployments should evaluate the OpenHuman architecture as a pattern, even if the product itself is consumer-grade.
